source_metadata.title_raw: Aaron Harp - A-19-201 - December 9, 2019 - Newport Beach

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Aaron Harp 
City Attorney 
City of Newport Beach 
100 Civic Center Dr. 
Newport Beach, CA 92660 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-19-201 
Dear Mr. Harp: 
December 9, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the City of Newport Beach 
("City") and City Councilmember Marshall "Duffy" Duffield, regarding the Political Reform Act 
("Act") and Government Code Section 1090, et seq. 1 Please note that we are only providing advice 
under the Act and Section 1090, not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as 
common law conflict of interest, including Public Contract Code. 
Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
We are required to forward your request regarding Section 1090 and all pertinent facts 
relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Orange County District Attorney's 
Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.l(c)(3).) We did not receive a written response from 
either entity. (Section 1097.l(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, for purposes of 
Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any 
individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.l(c)(S).) 
QUESTIONS 
1. May Councilmember Duffield donate the storage sheds to the City? 
2. If Councilmember Duffield donates the storage sheds to the City, may he participate in 
making future decisions related to the construction, maintenance, and operation of a temporary 
homeless shelter at the City's Corporate Yard ("Yard")? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 ofthe California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-19-201 
Page No. 2 
3. If Councilmember donates the storage sheds to the City, may he participate in making 
future decisions related to the homeless and/or a temporary homeless shelter at other locations in 
~c~ 
. 
CONCLUSION 
1-3. Section 1090 would not prohibit Councilmember Duffield from making a donation of 
storage sheds to the City. After making the donation in question, Councilmember Duffield would 
not be prohibited from participating in future decisions involving the City's temporary homeless 
shelter at the Yard, future decisions related to the homeless or temporary shelters at other locations 
in the City by virtue of the fact that he made this donation to the City. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
City of Newport Beach Councilmember Marshall "Duffy" Duffield 
Councilmember Duffield was first elected to the City Council of the City of Newport Beach 
in 2014, and is currently serving in his second term on the City Council. Councilmember Duffield 
owns a business which manufactures fiberglass storage sheds. 
City of Newport Beach Homeless Shelter Proposals 
The City is currently searching for a property to establish a temporary homeless shelter 
to house people experiencing homelessness. One location being considered by the City is the Yard 
located at 592 Superior A venue in Newport Beach. Should the City Council decide to establish the 
temporary homeless shelter at the Yard, Councilmember Duffield is considering donating 
approximately 30 to 40 storage sheds to provide shelter to those people experiencing homelessness. 
Pursuant to the policies adopted by the City Council, the City Council would be required to approve 
the donation of the storage sheds. However, no donation agreement would be required. 
Councilmember Duffield has already participated in City Council actions related to the 
homeless and the proposed temporary homeless shelter at the Yard. If Councilmember Duffield 
decides to donate the storage sheds to the City, he will not participate in the City Council action to 
approve or reject the donation. 
ANALYSIS 
Section I 090 
Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, 
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. Under Section 1090, "the prohibited 
act is the making of a contract in which the official has a financial interest" (People v. Honig (1996) 
48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333), and officials are deemed to have a financial interest in a contract if they · 
might profit from it in any way. (Id.) According to your facts, although the City Council will need 
to approve the donation, no donation agreement will be required. To the extent no agreement 
between the City and Councilmember Duffield is contemplated, Section 1090 would not apply to 
the present situation. 


The Act 
File No. A-19-201 
Page No. 3 
Section 87100 prohibits any state or local public official from making, participating in 
making, or using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has an interest specified in Section 87103. "Governmental decision" means any action taken 
by a government agency that has a financial effect on any person other than the governmental 
agency making the decision. (Regulation 18700(c)(2)(B)(4).) According to the facts presented, 
Councilmember Duffield anticipates making the donation of storage sheds in his personal capacity, 
and will not participate in the City Council action to approve or reject the donation. The Act only 
prohibits the Councilmember from making, participating in making or attempting to use his official 
position to influence a governmental decision in which he has a financial interest, and only then if 
his interest would be foreseeably and materially financially affected by the governmental decision. 
Based on the facts presented, there is no indication the City Council's future decisions regarding the 
Yard or other temporary homeless shelters would have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on 
any identified financial interests of Councilmember Duffield. Thus, no conflict of interest exists 
barring any other interests in the decision. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
ZWN:aja 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Zachary W. ·Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

1-3. Section 1090 would not prohibit Councilmember Duffield from making a donation of 
storage sheds to the City. After making the donation in question, Councilmember Duffield would 
not be prohibited from participating in future decisions involving the City's temporary homeless 
shelter at the Yard, future decisions related to the homeless or temporary shelters at other locations 
in the City by virtue of the fact that he made this donation to the City.
